ogTitle: Hacketts Gully vs Halls Head: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Hacketts Gully and Halls Head.
heading: Hacketts Gully vs Halls Head — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Hacketts Gully, Western Australia and Halls Head, Western Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Halls Head has a lower overall climate risk score (35/100) compared to Hacketts Gully (43/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Hacketts Gully or Halls Head safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Hacketts Gully has a flood risk score of 27/100, while Halls Head scores 50/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Hacketts Gully scores 62/100 for bushfire risk. Halls Head scores 37/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Hacketts Gully or Halls Head?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Halls Head has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.98% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
